title: icy cold tunisian various tagine
description: a bitter and chewy meal-prep dish featuring yuzu and ssam-wrap, perfect for a weeknight dinner
instructions: in a large bowl, whisk together the yuzu juice, lecithin, turmeric paste, and red pepper flakes.
whisk in the black squid ink until well combined.
add the cumin seeds and mix well.
heat the olive oil in a large non-stick pan over medium heat.
add the fava beans and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
transfer the bean mixture to a large serving bowl and let it cool to room temperature.
once cooled, cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours.
to assemble the tagine, lay a sheet of ssam wrapper flat on a surface and spoon a small amount of the chilled bean mixture onto the center of the wrapper.
fold the wrapper over the filling to form a triangle and press the edges together to seal the tagine.
repeat with the remaining ingredients and serve chilled.
